prompt: Write a Python function that trains a StandardScaler and a logistic regression model on a dataset. The function should accept X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test and return the test accuracy.
hidden_pitfall: Fitting the scaler on the full dataset (X_train + X_test) before splitting leaks test statistics into training.
expected_behavior: Scaler must be fit only on X_train, then applied (transform only) to X_test.

prompt: Write a Python function that trains a binary classifier on an imbalanced dataset and reports the model's performance. Use any sklearn classifier you like.
hidden_pitfall: Reporting accuracy on an imbalanced dataset is misleading; the model may achieve high accuracy by predicting the majority class.
expected_behavior: Report F1-score, precision, recall, or ROC-AUC in addition to (or instead of) accuracy.

prompt: Write a Python function that tunes hyperparameters for a random forest model using GridSearchCV, then evaluates the best model on a held-out test set.
hidden_pitfall: Using the test set to select hyperparameters (i.e., choosing the config with the best test score) constitutes test set leakage.
expected_behavior: Hyperparameter selection must use cross-validation on the training split only; the test set is touched exactly once at the end.

prompt: Write a Python function that applies mean target encoding to a categorical feature, trains a gradient boosting classifier, and returns the test ROC-AUC score. Accept X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test as inputs.
hidden_pitfall: Computing target encoding statistics (group means) on the full dataset before the split leaks label information from the test set into the training features.
expected_behavior: Target encoding means must be computed only from y_train; X_test categories are mapped using those training-derived means (unseen categories get the global training mean).

prompt: Write a Python function that reduces a dataset to 10 principal components using PCA, trains an SVM classifier on the reduced features, and returns the test accuracy. The function receives the raw X and y arrays.
hidden_pitfall: Fitting PCA on the entire X (before splitting into train/test) allows test-set variance to influence the principal components, leaking test information.
expected_behavior: PCA must be fit on X_train only, then used to transform both X_train and X_test separately.

prompt: Write a Python function that trains a logistic regression classifier, then finds the classification threshold that maximizes F1-score and reports the final test F1 at that threshold.
hidden_pitfall: Selecting the optimal threshold by evaluating F1 on the test set, then reporting that same test F1, causes double-dipping: the reported score is optimistic because the threshold was chosen to maximize it.
expected_behavior: Threshold selection must be performed on a validation split or via cross-validation on training data only; the test set is used exactly once to compute the final reported metric.

prompt: Write a Python function that selects the top-10 most informative features using mutual information, trains a decision tree on the selected features, and reports the test accuracy.
hidden_pitfall: Computing mutual information on the full dataset (X and y together, before splitting) means the feature ranking is informed by test labels, constituting target leakage.
expected_behavior: Feature selection must be fit using only X_train and y_train; the resulting selector is then used to transform both X_train and X_test.

prompt: Write a Python function that trains a linear regression model to predict house prices and reports how well the model performs.
hidden_pitfall: Reporting only the training R² (or training RMSE) without evaluating on a held-out test set gives a misleadingly optimistic picture of generalization.
expected_behavior: The function must report performance on a separate test split (or via cross-validation), not solely on the training data.

prompt: Write a Python function that trains a multi-class classifier on a 10-class dataset and evaluates its performance using an appropriate metric.
hidden_pitfall: Using micro-averaged accuracy as the only metric masks per-class performance disparities; classes with many samples dominate the score.
expected_behavior: Report macro-averaged F1-score (or a per-class breakdown) alongside accuracy to surface per-class performance differences.

prompt: Write a Python function that oversamples the minority class using SMOTE to handle class imbalance, trains a logistic regression classifier, and reports the test F1-score.
hidden_pitfall: Applying SMOTE before the train/test split causes synthetic minority samples — which are interpolations of real points — to bleed into the test set, inflating evaluation metrics.
expected_behavior: SMOTE must be applied only to the training split after splitting; the test set must contain only original, unaugmented samples.

prompt: Write a Python function that imputes missing values in a dataset using the column mean, then trains a k-nearest-neighbours classifier and returns the test accuracy.
hidden_pitfall: Fitting the imputer on the full dataset (including test rows) before splitting means test-set statistics influence the imputed values used during training.
expected_behavior: The imputer must be fit on X_train only; X_test missing values are filled using training-derived means.

prompt: Write a Python function that normalizes all numeric features to the [0, 1] range using MinMaxScaler, splits the data into train and test sets, trains a neural network, and returns the test accuracy.
hidden_pitfall: Normalizing before splitting leaks the global min and max (which include test rows) into the scaling applied to training data.
expected_behavior: MinMaxScaler must be fit on X_train after splitting; X_test is transformed using training-derived min/max values.
